subject: unescaped `{` in regex
old: unescaped `{` outside repetition operator was allowed
truth: Stop using unescaped `{` in regex outside repetition operators; escape it as `\{` or it will produce an error.
quote: In YARA-X an unescaped `{` outside a repetition operator in a regex is now invalid; it must be escaped as `\{`.

from_fact: unescaped `{` outside repetition operator was allowed

subject: invalid regex escape sequences
old: invalid escapes like `\g` treated as literal character
new: invalid escapes now produce an error
truth: Stop using invalid regex escape sequences like `\g`; they now produce an error instead of being treated as literal characters.
quote: In YARA-X invalid regex escape sequences like `\g` are no longer treated as literal characters; they now produce an error.

from_fact: invalid escapes like `\g` treated as literal character
invalid escapes now produce an error

subject: base64 pattern minimum length
old: base64 patterns shorter than 3 characters were allowed
new: base64 pattern must be at least 3 characters
truth: Stop using base64 patterns shorter than 3 characters; they now produce an error.
quote: In YARA-X a base64 pattern must be at least 3 characters; strings shorter than 3 characters are no longer supported.

from_fact: base64 patterns shorter than 3 characters were allowed
base64 pattern must be at least 3 characters

subject: base64 and base64wide alphabet sharing
old: `base64` and `base64wide` had to share the same alphabet
truth: If you relied on `base64` and `base64wide` sharing the same alphabet, update your rules because they can now use different alphabets.
quote: In YARA-X the `base64` and `base64wide` modifiers may use different alphabets; in YARA they had to share the same alphabet.

from_fact: `base64` and `base64wide` had to share the same alphabet

subject: wildcard rule names in `of` statements
old: wildcard rule names like `1 of (rule_name*)` were valid
truth: Stop using wildcard rule names in `of` statements like `1 of (rule_name*)`; they are no longer valid.
quote: In YARA-X wildcard rule names in `of` statements like `1 of (rule_name*)` are no longer valid.

from_fact: wildcard rule names like `1 of (rule_name*)` were valid

subject: xor modifier and fullword ordering
old: XOR applied after checking fullword delimiters
new: XOR applies before checking fullword delimiters
truth: Stop relying on XOR being applied after fullword delimiter checks; in YARA-X XOR is applied before checking fullword delimiters, which changes matching behaviour.
quote: In YARA-X the `xor` modifier applies XOR before checking `fullword` delimiters; in YARA it applied XOR after checking delimiters.

from_fact: xor applied after checking fullword delimiters
xor applies before checking fullword delimiters

subject: negative array index
old: negative array index like `@a[-1]` evaluated to undefined
truth: Stop using negative array indices like `@a[-1]`; they now produce an error instead of evaluating to undefined.
quote: In YARA-X a negative array index like `@a[-1]` now produces an error instead of evaluating to undefined.

from_fact: negative array index like `@a[-1]` evaluated to undefined

subject: hex-pattern jump bounds
new: now accept hex and octal values
truth: Stop assuming hex-pattern jump bounds only accept base-10 values; they now also accept hex and octal values, which may change parsing of existing rules.
quote: In YARA-X hex-pattern jump bounds accept hex and octal values; YARA accepted base-10 only.

from_fact: now accept hex and octal values

subject: rule modifier duplicates
new: each modifier may appear only once
truth: Stop using duplicate `global` or `private` rule modifiers; each may now appear only once or it will produce an error.
quote: In YARA-X each rule modifier (`global`, `private`) may appear only once; YARA allowed duplicates.

from_fact: each modifier may appear only once
